title: Hunter-Killer probot
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Otheruses|Hunter-killer}} {{StarshipClass |image=[[File:HKprobot-NEGTD.png]] |name=Hunter-Killer probot |designer= |manufacturer=[[Arakyd Industries/Legends|Arakyd Industries]]''[[Dark Empire Sourcebook]]'' |line=[[Arakyd Probot Series]]''[[The New Essential Guide to Droids]]'' |model=Hunter-Killer probot |type=[[Probe droid/Legends|Probe droid]] |cost=165,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] |modifier= |sysmods= |length= |width= |height=150 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] tall |mass= |max accel= |mglt= |max speed=300 km/h |maneuverability= |engine= |hyperdrive= |hdrange= |hdsystem=None{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20050414planet1|series=Byss and the Deep Core|text=Part 1: Byss}} |poweroutput= |power= |shield gen=[[Deflector shield generator/Legends|Equipped]] |hull=[[Hull/Legends|Equipped]] |sensor=[[Hullscanner Detection Array]]''[[The Essential Guide to Droids]]'' *Passive *Scan *Search *Focus |target= |navigation= |avionics= |maincomp= |countermeasures= |armament=*[[Ion cannon/Legends|Ion cannons]] (2) *[[Quad turbolaser cannon/Legends|Quad]] [[Blaster cannon/Legends|blaster cannons]] (2) *[[Tractor beam/Legends|Tractor beam]] [[Tractor beam projector/Legends|projector]] (1) |complement= |bays=Internal secure chamber |escapepods= |crew=Integrated [[Droid/Legends|droid]] intelligence |skeleton= |passengers=0 |capacity=200 [[Metric ton/Legends|metric tons]] |cargohandling= |consumables=None |lifesupport= |communications= |othersystems=*Heavy grasping extensors (4) *Fine work grasping extensors (4) |availability=Not available for public sale |role=*[[Sentry droid/Legends|Patrol droid]] *Detainment |firstuse= |retired= |affiliation=*[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]][[Dark Empire 5|''Dark Empire'' 5]] *[[Dark Empire]] **[[Byss Security]] *[[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] {{C|reprogrammed}} }} {{Quote|Uh-oh...probe's checkn' out the ''marauder''!''
''That ain't no ordinary patrol droid...that's an Imperial ''Hunter-Killer''!|Shug Ninx and Salla Zend|}} The '''Hunter-Killer probot''', also known as the '''H-K probot''', was a bizarre evolution of the more common [[Viper probe droid/Legends|Viper probe droid]]. Produced by [[Arakyd Industries/Legends|Arakyd Laboratories]], they were commonly used as autonomous patrol vehicles near [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Planet/Legends|worlds]]. ==Characteristics== {{Quote|S'closin' on us ''fast'', shug. Hit him! Hit him!''
''H-K droid's got some kind of ''defectors''! The Falcon's missiles are bouncin' off his shields like ''pebbles''!|Salla Zend and Shug Ninx, after engaging Judgement 12-X7|}} [[File:HKprobot_egtd.png|thumb|left|150px|Hunter-Killer [[:Category:Technical drawing images|schematics]].]] Shaped roughly like its design predecessor, the Hunter-Killer was a technological monstrosity scaled up to [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ship]]-size. The interior of each [[Droid/Legends|droid]] was designed to be a detainment platform for captured [[Starship/Legends|starships]], with [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor beam]] projectors covering the droid's outer hull, ready to drag wayward ships into one of several internal docking bays. Following capture, prisoners would be held inside the droid, until the Imperial officer in charge was notified of the capture and had determined the appropriate course of action. They were often used with [[Imperial Customs Frigate]]s and [[Guardian-class patrol ship|''Guardian''-class patrol ships]]. However, like capital-ships, it possessed a design flaw: Someone imprisoned could gain access and hijack a Hunter-Killer probot via its control room and thus it created the risk of it turning against the Empire. ==History== {{Quote|Judgement 12-X7 to security command... apprehension and detainment of YT-1300 quarry in progress.|Judgement 12-X7, an HK probot stationed on Byss moving to detain the ''Millennium Falcon''.|}} [[File:EmperorsCitadelTEA.jpg|thumb|left|150px|A Hunter-Killer probot around the [[Emperor's Citadel]] on [[Byss/Legends|Byss]].]] After the Viper probe droids proved successful at locating [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] bases, Arakyd gambled by producing a massive 150 meter droid. The H-K found great favor in the Empire, used to capture [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]] and Rebel ships. A number of Hunter-Killers were stationed around [[Byss/Legends|Byss]] after [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] return. A notable flaw in the design was exploited when smugglers [[Shug Ninx]] and [[Salla Zend]] were captured by the HK probot [[Judgement 12-X7]] while aiding [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] agents infiltrate Byss. After being detained within Judgement 12-X7 while piloting the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|Millennium Falcon]] to avoid Imperial detection, Shug and Salla were able to hot-wire the droid, taking control of the probot's circuitry and using it to rescue [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]], and [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]] from an Imperial prison. However, while attempting to flee, the escaping Republic prisoners were spotted by an Imperial patrol and in response Shug turned the probot's attack systems against the patrol, resulting in a firefight with the prison's own security forces. While the Imperials engaged the rogue probot Shug, Salla, Solo, Chewbacca, and Leia all boarded the Millennium Falcon which was still docked within Judgement 12-X7 alongside a [[Dopplegänger|Force projection]] of [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]]. Taking off from within the reprogrammed probot, the Millennium Falcon and its crew managed to escape Byss just as the prison's [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolasers]] found their mark and destroyed Judgement 12-X7. ==Appearances== *{{WEGCite|story=Raid on Brentaal|book=The Far Orbit Project}} *[[Dark Empire (audio drama)|''Dark Empire'' audio drama]] *[[Dark Empire 5|''Dark Empire'' 5]] {{1st}}
